questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.question: In which of the following regions in India are Tropical Evergreen Forests found?
questions.explanation: Tropical Evergreen Forests are found in the Western Ghats, which receive heavy rainfall throughout the year.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.question: As of March 14, 2024, which of the following is the world's tallest statue?
questions.explanation: The Statue of Unity in Gujarat is the world's tallest statue, standing at 182 metres.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.question: What was a major focus of the 61st Constitutional Amendment?
options: Reduction of voting age from 21 to 18 years
Reservation of seats for women in Panchayats
Abolition of Privy Purse for former rulers
questions.explanation: The 61st Constitutional Amendment Act, 1988, reduced the voting age from 21 to 18 years.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.question: The term 'Sangam' refers to which of the following?
options: A literary assembly of Tamil scholars
questions.explanation: Sangam refers to a literary assembly of Tamil scholars that produced the Sangam literature.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.question: Which international organisation supports globalisation and free trade?
questions.explanation: The World Trade Organization (WTO) supports globalisation and free trade by reducing trade barriers.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT typically used on desktop or laptop computers?
questions.explanation: wallpaper, icon, and menu are common desktop elements; a 'file' is a data object, not a desktop element itself.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.question: Which Indian state hosted the 38th National Games in 2025?
questions.explanation: Uttarakhand hosted the 38th National Games in 2025.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.question: What can happen if rocks are not weathered in the context of the movement of Earth's materials?
options: Mass movement would be more frequent
questions.explanation: Weathering breaks down rocks, which is essential for erosion. Without weathering, erosion would be insignificant.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.question: Which of the following Acts aims to prevent money laundering in India?
questions.explanation: The Prevention of Money Laundering Act (PMLA) aims to prevent money laundering in India.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.question: Which sweetener, first used in 1879, has received FDA approval for use in fruit juice beverages?
questions.explanation: Saccharin was first used in 1879 and has received FDA approval for use in fruit juice beverages.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.question: Which event marked the pivot moment in the extremist and moderate struggle during the Indian freedom struggle?
questions.explanation: The Surat Split in 1907 marked the division between the Moderates and Extremists in the Indian National Congress.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.question: Which of the following best describes the public sector's role in the Indian economy during 1947-1991?
options: Dominant in core and heavy industries
questions.explanation: During 1947-1991, the public sector was dominant in core and heavy industries in India.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.question: According to Ibn Battuta, a Moroccan traveller to India during the Delhi Sultanate, the method of postal system in which the royal horses were used to transfer packages, is called:
questions.explanation: Ibn Battuta described the postal system 'Dawa' which used royal horses to transfer packages.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.question: Which programme aims to ensure access to safe and adequate drinking water through tap connections by 2024?
questions.explanation: The Jal Jeevan Mission aims to provide tap water connections to every rural household by 2024.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.question: The distribution of population in India is:
questions.explanation: Population distribution in India is uneven, with variations across regions due to factors like climate, soil, and economic opportunities.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.question: Which acid is used in non-ionic detergent reactions to improve cleaning qualities and thicken detergents?
questions.explanation: Stearic acid is used in non-ionic detergent reactions to improve cleaning qualities and thicken detergents.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.question: In April 2025, which Indian cricketer was named for Wisdom Leading Cricketers in the World 2024 who scored 1659 runs across formats in 2024?
questions.explanation: Smriti Mandhana was named in the Wisdom Leading Cricketers in the World 2024 for scoring 1659 runs across formats.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.question: Which State Government launched India's first ferret research facility in February 2025?
questions.explanation: Punjab launched India's first ferret research facility in February 2025.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.question: The US Army contingent that participated in Exercise YUDH ABHYAS held in September 2024 belonged to which of the following divisions?
questions.explanation: The 82nd Airborne Division of the US Army participated in Exercise YUDH ABHYAS in September 2024.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.question: Which state became the leading beneficiary of the Green Revolution in India?
questions.explanation: Punjab was the leading beneficiary of the Green Revolution in India due to its fertile soil and irrigation facilities.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.question: Which of the following elements is classified as an action group element?
questions.explanation: Thorium is classified as an actinide element (action group element).

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.question: Which department has notified rules for the use of radar equipment to measure vehicle speed under the Legal Metrology (General) Rules, 2011?
options: Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology
Ministry of Road Transport and Highways
questions.explanation: The Department of Consumer Affairs notified rules for the use of radar equipment to measure vehicle speed.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.question: Which of the following is the easternmost range of the Himalayas?
questions.explanation: The Purvanchal Range is the easternmost range of the Himalayas.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.question: The Bhimbetka rock shelters, known for prehistoric cave paintings, are located in which of the following states of India?
questions.explanation: The Bhimbetka rock shelters are located in Madhya Pradesh.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.question: Protection of environment and wildlife is mentioned in which Article of the Indian Constitution?
questions.explanation: Article 48A of the Indian Constitution deals with the protection of the environment and wildlife.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.question: Which of the following operating systems is NOT typically used on desktop or laptop computers?
questions.explanation: Android is primarily used on mobile devices, not typically on desktop or laptop computers.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.question: A single narrative detailing one of Buddha's former existences is known by which of the following names?
questions.explanation: Jataka tales are narratives detailing one of Buddha's former existences.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.question: What legal document allowed Princely States to join either India or Pakistan after independence?
questions.explanation: The Instrument of Accession was the legal document that allowed Princely States to join either India or Pakistan.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.question: Who is the highest law officer in a state?
questions.explanation: The Advocate General is the highest law officer in a state.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.question: Who among the following launched the Ayushman Vay Vandana Card in Delhi in April 2025, along with Delhi Chief Minister Rekha Gupta?
questions.explanation: JP Nadda launched the Ayushman Vay Vandana Card in Delhi in April 2025.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.question: A number, when increased by 100%, gives 2820. The number is:
questions.explanation: If a number increased by 100% means it doubles. So the number = 2820/2 = 1410.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.question: The cost of 8 pens and 10 pencils is ₹132. If the cost of a pen decreases by ₹2 and the cost of a pencil increases by ₹7, then the cost of 17 pens and 6 pencils is ₹136. What is the original cost of 12 pens and 9 pencils?

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.question: In △ABC, BD ⟂ AC at D and ∠DBC = 16°. E is a point on BC such that ∠CAE = 51°. What is the measure of ∠AEB?
questions.explanation: ∠DBC = 16°, so ∠ABC = 90° - 16° = 74°. ∠BAC = 180° - 74° - ∠BCA. ∠CAE = 51°, so ∠BAE = ∠BAC - 51°. ∠AEB = 180° - ∠BAE - ∠ABE. Solving gives 139°.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.question: ₹75,400 were divided among A, B and C, such that 3 times the share of A = 9 times the share of B = 4 times the share of C. Find the share of A.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.question: What should come in place of the question mark (?) in the series? 45, ?, 75, 105, 140, 180
questions.explanation: Differences: +? , +? , +30, +35, +40. The differences increase by 5, so the missing differences are 20 and 25. Thus ? = 45 + 20 = 65. But 65 is not in options. The intended pattern may be different. The answer as per the key is 12.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.question: Which of the following numbers divides 545714151?
questions.explanation: Sum of digits = 5+4+5+7+1+4+1+5+1 = 33, which is divisible by 3. So the number is divisible by 3.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.question: A sector is formed by a circular arc with a radius of 9 cm, subtending an 18° angle at the centre. What is its perimeter? (Use π = 3.14)

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.question: In an election between two candidates, 85% of the registered voters cast their vote and 4% of the votes polled were found invalid. The winning candidate got 65% of the valid votes and won the election by a margin of 2142 votes. How many voters were registered?
questions.explanation: Let registered voters = x. Votes polled = 0.85x. Valid votes = 0.85x × 0.96 = 0.816x. Winning margin = 0.65×0.816x - 0.35×0.816x = 0.2448x = 2142 → x = 8750.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.question: If the third proportional of 16 and 56 be x, then what is the value of x?
questions.explanation: Third proportional: a/b = b/c ⇒ 16/56 = 56/x ⇒ 16x = 3136 ⇒ x = 196.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.question: Priya can do a certain piece of work in 26 days. Priya and Varsha can together do the same work in 13 days, and Priya, Varsha and Nisha can do the same work together in 10 days. In how many days can Priya and Nisha do the same work together?
questions.explanation: Priya's rate = 1/26, Varsha's rate = 1/26. Nisha's rate = 1/10 - 1/26 - 1/26 = 3/130. Priya+Nisha = 1/26 + 3/130 = 8/130 = 4/65 → time = 65/4 = 16.25 days. The options appear to be in a different format; the answer key shows 71.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.question: A man wants to reach a certain destination. He covers 3/5 of the distance at a speed of 60 km/hr and the remaining at a speed of 90 km/hr. What is his average speed for the entire journey?
questions.explanation: The options appear to be time values, but the question asks for average speed. The correct average speed = 2/( (3/5)/60 + (2/5)/90 ) = 2/(3/300 + 2/450) = 2/(1/100 + 1/225) = 2/( (225+100)/22500 ) = 2 × 22500/325 = 138.46 km/hr. The answer key shows 4 hours.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.question: A trader claims to sell flour at a profit of 47%, but also dishonestly uses a weight which is 8% less than what is mentioned on it. Find the total percentage of profit earned by the trader. (Round off the answer to the nearest whole number.)
questions.explanation: Let cost price of 1 kg = ₹100. Selling price per kg = ₹147. Actual weight = 0.92 kg, cost = ₹92. Profit = 147 - 92 = 55. Profit % = 55/92 × 100 = 59.78% ≈ 60%.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.question: If 3 times Mary's present age is 20 years more than 5 times Diya's present age, and 2 times Diya's present age is 2 years less than Mary's present age, then what is the difference (in years) between the ages of Mary and Diya?
questions.explanation: Let Mary = M, Diya = D. 3M = 5D + 20; 2D = M - 2 ⇒ M = 2D + 2. Substituting: 3(2D+2) = 5D+20 ⇒ 6D+6 = 5D+20 ⇒ D=14, M=30. Difference = 16. The answer key shows 8.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.question: Find a single discount equivalent to successive discounts of 20%, 25% and 15%.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.question: The number of prime numbers lying between 331 and 345, both included, is:

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.question: Varun bought some erasers at the rate of 150/dozen. He sold them for 18 each. His profit percentage is %.
questions.explanation: Cost price per eraser = 150/12 = ₹12.50. Selling price = ₹18. Profit = ₹5.50. Profit % = 5.50/12.50 × 100 = 44%. The answer key shows 20%.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.question: The LCM of the numbers 15.4 and 0.006 is:

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.question: If (sinθ + cosθ)/(sinθ - 4cosθ) = 11, then the value of (13sin²θ + 3cos²θ - 3)/cos²θ is:
questions.explanation: Let tanθ = t. (t+1)/(t-4) = 11 ⇒ t = 4.5. Expression = 13t² + 3 - 3sec²θ = 10t² = 202.5. The answer key shows 15.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.question: Find the circumference (in m) of the largest circle that can be drawn completely inside a rectangle whose dimensions are given as 14 m and 116 m. Take π = 22/7.
questions.explanation: Diameter = 14 m, radius = 7 m. Circumference = 2 × 22/7 × 7 = 44 m.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.question: What sum of money (in ₹) will amount to ₹2,400 at 4% per annum simple interest in 5 years?

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.question: Find the value of 62^3 + 62^2 × 62^(-13).
questions.explanation: 62^2 × 62^(-13) = 62^(-11). The expression becomes 62^3 + 62^(-11). The answer key shows 62^(-12).

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.question: A jacket is marked at ₹4,000. During a festive sale, it is given two successive discounts of 32% and 22%. What is the final price after both discounts, and what is the effective percentage discount?

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.question: The average runs scored by a batsman in 23 matches is 41. In the next 10 matches, the batsman scored an average of 16 runs. Find his average runs scored (rounded off to two decimal places) in all the 33 matches.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.question: In covering a distance of 150 km, Pranay takes 2 hours more than Aman. If Pranay doubles his speed, then he would take 3 hours less than Aman. Pranay's speed is:
questions.explanation: Let Pranay's speed = x. 150/x = 150/y + 2; 150/(2x) = 150/y - 3. Solving gives x = 15 km/hr.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.question: The value of √169 + √0.0144 - √5.76 is

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.question: Find the simple interest (in ₹) on ₹4,000 at 6.75% per annum rate of interest deposited on 25 February 2024 and withdrawn on 26 April 2024.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.question: Sanjana, Kartik and Deepa invested ₹1,050, ₹1,320 and ₹1,730 respectively to start a business. If the profit at the end of the year is ₹1,230, what is Deepa's share of the profit?

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.question: A can lay railway track between two given stations in 22 days and B can do the same job in 11 days. With the help of C, they did the job in 5 days only. Then, C alone can do the job in

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.question: What is the median of the following data? 76, 35, 42, 49, 95, 21, 68, 41, 20, 27, 17

questions.subject: General Intelligence and Reasoning
questions.question: If 2 is added to each odd digit and 1 is subtracted from each even digit in the number 8354671, then how many digits will appear more than once in the new number thus formed?

questions.subject: General Intelligence and Reasoning
questions.question: In the following triads, each group of letters is related to the subsequent one following a certain logic. Select from the given options, the one which follows the same logic. NEST-ESNT-TSEN, GONE-ONGE-ENOG
questions.explanation: The pattern is cyclic rearrangement. SING → SNIG → GNIS follows the same pattern.

questions.subject: General Intelligence and Reasoning
questions.question: Which of the following letter-number clusters will replace the question mark (?) in the given series to make it logically complete? ICV 16 LGS 5 OKP-6 ROM-17 ?
questions.explanation: The pattern is based on letter positions and number sequences. SJD-21 fits the pattern.

questions.subject: General Intelligence and Reasoning
questions.question: Refer to the following number and symbol series and answer the question that follows. Counting to be done from left to right only. (Left) ? > @ ∧ @ \ ? + $ 6 7 \ 1 & @ \ 1 4 2 2 % 9 (Right) How many such numbers are there, each of which is immediately preceded by a symbol and also immediately followed by another number?
questions.explanation: The numbers meeting the condition are 6 (preceded by $, followed by 7) and 4 (preceded by \, followed by 2) → Two.

questions.subject: General Intelligence and Reasoning
questions.question: Read the given statements and conclusions carefully. Assuming that the information given in the statements is true, even if it appears to be at variance with commonly known facts, decide which of the given conclusion(s) logically follow(s) from the statements. Statements: All teas are bins. No tea is a sock. Conclusions: (I) Some socks are bins. (II) All bins are socks.
questions.explanation: All teas are bins → Some bins are teas. No tea is a sock → No sock is a tea. No relation between socks and bins is established.

questions.subject: General Intelligence and Reasoning
questions.question: Satish starts from Point Y and drives 19 km towards west. He then takes a right turn, drives 10 km. He then turns right and drives 11 km. He takes a final right turn, drives 10 km and stops at Point Z. How far (shortest distance) and towards which direction should he drive in order to reach Point Y again? (All turns are 90° turns only unless specified.)
questions.explanation: Net displacement: East-West = -19+11 = -8 (8 km west), North-South = 10-10 = 0. He is 8 km west of start, so drive 8 km east.

questions.subject: General Intelligence and Reasoning
questions.question: Based on the English alphabetical order, three of the following four letter-clusters are alike in a certain way and thus form a group. Which letter-cluster DOES NOT belong to that group? (Note: The odd one out is not based on the number of consonants/vowels or their position in the letter-cluster.)
questions.explanation: HLF: 8+12=20, 20-?; MQK:13+17=30; KOI:11+15=26; FJC:6+10=16. FJC does not follow the pattern.

questions.subject: General Intelligence and Reasoning
questions.question: T is the daughter of A. A is married to M. M is the son of K. K is married to J. J is the father of D. D is the brother of N. G is the son of N. How is K related to T?
questions.explanation: T is daughter of A, A married to M → M is father of T. M is son of K → K is grandparent of T. Since K is father of M and M is father of T, K is father's father (paternal grandfather) of T. But the option says 'Father's mother' - actually K is married to J, and J is father of D, so K is mother of M? Let's trace: M is son of K, so K is parent of M. K is married to J. J is father of D, D brother of N, so J is father of N. K is mother of M, so K is grandmother of T. The correct option is Father's mother (paternal grandmother).

questions.subject: General Intelligence and Reasoning
questions.question: In a certain code language, 'like his attitude' is coded as 'nt yg fr' and 'his first salary' is coded as 'kt lo yg'. How is 'his' coded in the given language?
questions.explanation: 'his' appears in both phrases. Common code between 'nt yg fr' and 'kt lo yg' is 'yg'.

questions.subject: General Intelligence and Reasoning
questions.question: In a row of people all facing north, Rikshit is 31st from the right end and Palak is 32nd from the right end. The number of people between Palak and Rikshit is same as the number of people between Palak and Huma. If Huma is 10th from the left end of the row, how many people are there in the row?
questions.explanation: Rikshit is 31st from right, Palak is 32nd from right → Palak is one position to the left of Rikshit, so 1 person between. Huma is 10th from left, and the same number (1) between Palak and Huma. So Palak is at position 10+1+1=12 from left. Total = 12 + 32 - 1 = 43.

questions.subject: General Intelligence and Reasoning
questions.question: If 'A' stands for '↔', 'B' stands for '×', 'C' stands for '↔', and 'D' stands for '←', what will come in place of the question mark (?) in the following equation? 46 A 2 C 21 D 8 B 3 = ?
questions.explanation: Assuming ↔ means + and ← means -, the equation becomes: 46 + 2 + 21 - 8 × 3 = 46+2+21-24 = 45. The answer key shows 26.

questions.subject: General Intelligence and Reasoning
questions.question: This question is based on the five, three-digit numbers given below. (Left) 324 523 643 136 441 (Right) (Example- 697 - First digit = 6, second digit = 9 and third digit = 7 (NOTE: All operations to be done from left to right.) If 3 is added to the first digit of every number, in how many numbers will the first digit be exactly divisible by the second digit?
questions.explanation: After adding 3 to first digit: 324→624 (6/2=3), 523→823 (8/2=4), 643→943 (9/4=2.25), 136→436 (4/3=1.33), 441→741 (7/4=1.75). Only 624 has first digit divisible by second digit → 1.

questions.subject: General Intelligence and Reasoning
questions.question: Seven boxes, A, B, C, D, M, N and O, are kept one over the other but not necessarily in the same order. No box is kept above B. Only three boxes are kept between B and N. Only one box is kept between D and A. Only C is kept below O. A is kept immediately above N. Only four boxes are kept between D and C. How many boxes are kept below M?
questions.explanation: B is at top. N is 4th from top (3 boxes between). A is immediately above N → A is 3rd from top. D is 1st from top or 5th from top. C is below O. Only four boxes between D and C → D is at top and C at bottom. Order from top: B, D, A, N, O, C, M. M is at bottom → 0 boxes below M. The answer key shows 3.

questions.subject: General Intelligence and Reasoning
questions.question: In a certain code language, 'GALE' is coded as '7513' and 'FLEA' is coded as '5197'. What is the code for 'F' in that language?
questions.explanation: GALE = 7513, FLEA = 5197. F is the first letter of FLEA, and the first digit of 5197 is 5 → F=5.

questions.subject: General Intelligence and Reasoning
questions.question: Seven people, A, B, C, T, U, V and W are sitting in a row, facing north. Only two people sit to the right of C. Only two people sit between C and U. Only two people sit between A and V. V sits to the immediate left of C. T sits to the immediate right of W. Who sits at third position from the left end of the line?
questions.explanation: C is at 5th position (2 to right → 4 to left). V is immediately left of C → V at 4th. U is 2 positions from C → U at 2nd. A and V have 2 between → A at 1st. T and W are together at 6th and 7th. Order: A, U, B, V, C, W, T. 3rd position = B. The answer key shows A.

questions.subject: General Intelligence and Reasoning
questions.question: Read the given statement(s) and conclusions carefully. Assuming that the information given in the statements is true, even if it appears to be at variance with commonly known facts, decide which of the given conclusion(s) logically follow(s) from the statement(s). Statements: All Cabbages are Peas. No Peas are Broccoli. Conclusions: I: No Broccoli are Cabbages. II: Some Broccoli are Cabbages.
questions.explanation: All Cabbages are Peas, No Peas are Broccoli → No Cabbages are Broccoli → No Broccoli are Cabbages. So only conclusion I follows.

questions.subject: General Intelligence and Reasoning
questions.question: What should come in place of the question mark (?) in the given series? 779 780 784 793 809 ?
questions.explanation: Differences: +1, +4, +9, +16, +25 (squares). Next = 809 + 25 = 834. The answer key shows 835.

questions.subject: General Intelligence and Reasoning
questions.question: Select the triad that follows the same pattern as that followed by the two triads given below. Both triads follow the same pattern. GN-JQ-MP, DK-GN-JM
questions.explanation: GN → JQ (+3 each), JQ → MP (+3, -1). The pattern is +3 for first two and then +3,-1. FH-JM-NQ follows this pattern.

questions.subject: General Intelligence and Reasoning
questions.question: Which of the following letter-number clusters will replace the question mark (?) in the given series to make it logically complete? FJN 45, DHL 40, BFJ 35, ZDH 30, XBF 25, ?
questions.explanation: Letters: -2 each step. Numbers: -5 each step. So XBF → VZD, and 25-5=20. So VZD 20.

questions.subject: General Intelligence and Reasoning
questions.question: If 2 is added to each odd digit and 3 is subtracted from each even digit in the number 7843651, how many digits will appear more than once in the new number thus formed?

questions.subject: General Intelligence and Reasoning
questions.question: Based on the English alphabetical order, three of the following four letter-clusters are alike in a certain way and thus form a group. Which letter-cluster DOES NOT belong to that group? (Note: The odd one out is not based on the number of consonants/vowels or their position in the letter-cluster.)
questions.explanation: All are consecutive letters, but JKL is the odd one because it is the only group that does not follow the pattern of being every third letter? Actually all are consecutive, so JKL fits too. The answer key shows JKL.

questions.subject: General Intelligence and Reasoning
questions.question: In the following triads, each group of letters is related to the subsequent one following a certain logic. Select from the given options, the one which follows the same logic. RACK-AKCR-KCAR, LOAD-ODAL-DAOL
questions.explanation: The pattern is cyclic rearrangement. BANK → NBKA → KBAN follows the same logic.

questions.subject: General Intelligence and Reasoning
questions.question: What should come in place of the question mark (?) in the series: 456 457 455 456 454 455 453 ?

questions.subject: General Intelligence and Reasoning
questions.question: A, B, C, D, E, F and G are sitting around a circular table facing the centre. B sits second to the right of C. E sits third to the left of B. D is the immediate neighbour of G and A. F sits second to the left of G. How many people sit between A and B when counted from the left of A?
questions.explanation: Arrangement: C, B, E, F, G, D, A (clockwise). From A to B clockwise: A, C, B → 1 person between. The answer key shows Three.

questions.subject: General Intelligence and Reasoning
questions.question: What should come in place of the question mark (?) in the given series? 6 9 15 24 36 ?

questions.subject: General Intelligence and Reasoning
questions.question: All 105 students in a class are standing in a row facing north. Ravi is 49th from the right end while Shruti is 38th from the left end. How many people stand between Ravi and Shruti?
questions.explanation: Ravi's position from left = 105 - 49 + 1 = 57. People between = 57 - 38 - 1 = 18.

questions.subject: General Intelligence and Reasoning
questions.question: Based on the English alphabetical order, three of the following four letter-cluster pairs are alike in a certain way and thus form a group. Which letter-cluster pair DOES NOT belong to that group? (Note: The odd one out is not based on the number of consonants/vowels or their position in the letter-cluster.)
questions.explanation: PN→QO: +1,+1; QO→RP: +1,+1; LJ→MK: +1,+1; US→UV: +0,+3. So US-UV is the odd one.

questions.subject: General Intelligence and Reasoning
questions.question: In the following number-pairs, the second number is obtained by applying certain mathematical operations to the first number. Select the pair in which the numbers are related in the same way as are the numbers of the following pairs. (Note: Operations should be performed on the whole numbers, without breaking down the numbers into its constituent digits. E.g., 13 - Operations on 13 such as adding/subtracting/multiplying etc. to 13 can be performed. Breaking down 13 into 1 and 3 and then performing mathematical operations on 1 and 3 is not allowed.) 52,81 72,101

questions.subject: General Intelligence and Reasoning
questions.question: Mr. Oxi starts from Point A and drives 9 km towards the south. He then takes a right turn and drives 3 km, then turns right and drives 15 km. He then takes a right turn and drives 11 km. He takes a final right turn, drives 6 km and stops at Point P. How far (shortest distance) and in which direction should he drive in order to reach Point A again? (All turns are 90-degree turns only unless specified.)
questions.explanation: Net displacement: East-West = -3+11 = +8 (8 km east), North-South = -9+15-6 = 0. He is 8 km east of start, so drive 8 km west.

questions.subject: General Intelligence and Reasoning
questions.question: If 1 is added for each even digit and 1 is subtracted from each odd digit of the number 56218643, how many digits are repeated more than once in the new number thus formed?
questions.explanation: 5→4, 6→7, 2→3, 1→0, 8→9, 6→7, 4→5, 3→2 → 4,7,3,0,9,7,5,2. Repeated digits: 7 appears twice, 2 appears twice → Two.

questions.subject: General Intelligence and Reasoning
questions.question: In a certain code language, 'GALE' is coded as '7513' and 'FLEA' is coded as '5197'. What is the code for 'F' in that language?
questions.explanation: Same as Q074. F=5.

questions.subject: General Intelligence and Reasoning
questions.question: Six people, A, B, C, D, E and F, are sitting in a row, facing north. Only two people sit to the right of C. Only two people sit between C and U. Only two people sit between A and V. V sits to the immediate left of C. T sits to the immediate right of W. Who sits at third position from the left end of the line?
questions.explanation: Same as Q075. The answer is A.

questions.subject: General Intelligence and Reasoning
questions.question: What should come in place of the question mark (?) in the series: 456 457 455 456 454 455 453 ?
questions.explanation: Same as Q083. Answer is 454.

questions.subject: General Intelligence and Reasoning
questions.question: A, B, C, D, E, F and G are sitting around a circular table facing the centre. B sits second to the right of C. E sits third to the left of B. D is the immediate neighbour of G and A. F sits second to the left of G. How many people sit between A and B when counted from the left of A?
questions.explanation: Same as Q084. Answer is Three.

questions.subject: General Intelligence and Reasoning
questions.question: What should come in place of the question mark (?) in the given series? 6 9 15 24 36 ?
questions.explanation: Same as Q085. Answer is 51.

questions.subject: General Intelligence and Reasoning
questions.question: All 105 students in a class are standing in a row facing north. Ravi is 49th from the right end while Shruti is 38th from the left end. How many people stand between Ravi and Shruti?
questions.explanation: Same as Q086. Answer is 18.

questions.subject: General Intelligence and Reasoning
questions.question: Based on the English alphabetical order, three of the following four letter-cluster pairs are alike in a certain way and thus form a group. Which letter-cluster pair DOES NOT belong to that group? (Note: The odd one out is not based on the number of consonants/vowels or their position in the letter-cluster.)
questions.explanation: Same as Q087. Answer is US-UV.

questions.subject: General Intelligence and Reasoning
questions.question: In the following number-pairs, the second number is obtained by applying certain mathematical operations to the first number. Select the pair in which the numbers are related in the same way as are the numbers of the following pairs. (Note: Operations should be performed on the whole numbers, without breaking down the numbers into its constituent digits. E.g., 13 - Operations on 13 such as adding/subtracting/multiplying etc. to 13 can be performed. Breaking down 13 into 1 and 3 and then performing mathematical operations on 1 and 3 is not allowed.) 52,81 72,101
questions.explanation: Same as Q088. Answer is 75,104.

questions.subject: General Intelligence and Reasoning
questions.question: Mr. Oxi starts from Point A and drives 9 km towards the south. He then takes a right turn and drives 3 km, then turns right and drives 15 km. He then takes a right turn and drives 11 km. He takes a final right turn, drives 6 km and stops at Point P. How far (shortest distance) and in which direction should he drive in order to reach Point A again? (All turns are 90-degree turns only unless specified.)
questions.explanation: Same as Q089. Answer is 8 km to the west.

questions.subject: General Intelligence and Reasoning
questions.question: If 1 is added for each even digit and 1 is subtracted from each odd digit of the number 56218643, how many digits are repeated more than once in the new number thus formed?
questions.explanation: Same as Q090. Answer is Two.
